channel.title: Insufficient on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions
channel.description: Recent content in Insufficient on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions

item.title: [Solution] Kubernetes Pod Stuck in Pending — insufficient resources
item.description:
A pod stuck in Pending means Kubernetes cannot schedule it onto any node. The scheduler cannot find a node with sufficient resources or matching node selectors to run the pod.

What This Error Means

When a pod remains in Pending state, the kube-scheduler has not assigned it to a node. Kubernetes continuously retries scheduling but fails because no node meets the pod’s requirements — whether due to insufficient CPU/memory, unmet node selectors, taints, or persistent volume constraints.

item.title: GCP Compute Engine: The Zone Does Not Have Enough Resources
item.description:
The The zone does not have enough resources error occurs when a Google Cloud zone has insufficient capacity for the requested resource (e.g., specific machine type, GPU, or SSD). This is a temporary capacity issue, not a quota limit.

Common Causes

  • The zone has a shortage of the requested machine type (e.g., n2-standard-8)
  • GPU resources are exhausted in the zone
  • SSD/local SSD capacity is fully allocated
  • High demand period (e.g., Black Friday) causes temporary capacity shortages

How to Fix

Try a different zone:
